messages.content:
Which of the following is an action of the parasympathetic nervous system?




A.
stimulation of digestion
B.
pupil dilation
C.
raised blood sugar levels
D.
accelerated heartbeat
A. stimulation of digestion

The amount of space devoted to each part of the body in the motor cortex is __. 



A.
proportional to the actual size of that part of the body
B.
the same for all body parts
C.
greater for your torso than for your hands
D.
proportional to the complexity and precision of movement of which that part of the body is capable
D. proportional to the complexity and precision of movement of which that part of the body is capable

Which of the following research methods allow(s) the researcher to draw cause– effect conclusions?



A.
correlational
B.
descriptive
C.
experimental
D.
all of the above
C. experimental

After learning the phone number for Pizza Hut, Bob cannot remember the phone number he learned last week for the Dunkin Donuts. After living in KL for 3 years, Jim is unable to remember his way around his hometown in which he had lived the previous 10 years prior to moving to KL. Bob is experiencing the effects of ____ interference, and Jim is experiencing the effects of __________interference.



A.
proactive; retroactive
B.
proactive; proactive
C.
retroactive; proactive
D.
retroactive; retroactive
A. proactive; retroactive

After learning the phone number for Pizza Hut, Bob cannot remember the phone number he learned last week for the Dunkin Donuts. After living in KL for 3 years, Jim is unable to remember his way around his hometown in which he had lived the previous 10 years prior to moving to KL. Bob is experiencing the effects of ____ interference, and Jim is experiencing the effects of __________interference.



A.
proactive; retroactive
B.
proactive; proactive
C.
retroactive; proactive
D.
retroactive; retroactive

A.
proactive; retroactive

Transduction of sound waves into neural impulses is performed by the _.



A.
eardrum
B.
oval window
C.
hair cells in the basilar membrane
D.
malleus, incus, and stapes
C. hair cells in the basilar membrane

Which of the following types of memory has the shortest duration?



A.
short-term memory
B.
episodic memory
C.
sensory memory
D.
semantic memory
C. sensory memory

Our short-term memory capacity is ___  ± 2 chunks.



A.
3
B.
7
C.
9
D.
5

In reinforcement, the probability of a behavior __; in punishment, the probability of a behavior ____.



A.
increases; decreases
B.
decreases; decreases
C.
decreases; increases
D.
increases; increases
A. increases; decreases

The actions of the pituitary gland are controlled by the __.



A.
hippocampus
B.
medulla
C.
hypothalamus
D.
amygdala
C. hypothalamus

Which of the following intelligence theorists proposed three types of intelligence —analytical, practical, and creative?



A.
Thurstone
B.
Gardner
C.
Spearman
D.
Sternberg
D. Sternberg

Which of the following would be the best procedure for obtaining a representative sample of the students at your school?



A.
sampling randomly among the students who belong to student clubs
B.
sampling randomly among students in the student union
C.
sampling randomly from a list of all the students enrolled at your school
D.
sampling randomly among students studying in the library
C. sampling randomly from a list of all the students enrolled at your school

Which of the following intelligence theorists emphasized the g factor?




A.
Thurstone
B.
Sternberg
C.
Gardner
D.
Spearman
D. Spearman
In a left-skewed distribution, the mean is _ than the median; in a right-skewed distribution, the mean is ___ than the median.
In a left-skewed distribution, the mean is less than the median; in a right-skewed distribution, the mean is greater than the median.

Which type of neuron carries information from the Central Nervous System to the Periphery Nervous System?



A.
motor neuron
B.
glial cell
C.
sensory neuron
D.
interneuron
A. motor neuron

Which of the following theories of forgetting argues that the forgotten information was in long-term memory but is no longer available?



A.
interference theory
B.
storage decay theory
C.
encoding failure theory
D.
cue-dependent theory
B. storage decay theory

The ________effect is a decrease in an intrinsically motivated behavior after the behavior is extrinsically reinforced and the reinforcement is discontinued.



A.
shaping
B.
partial reinforcement
C.
instinctual drift
D.
overjustification
D. overjustification

Continuing to take Panadol because it alleviates headaches is an example of _ , and no longer parking in “No Parking” zones because you lost money in fines for doing so is an example of __.



A.
positive reinforcement; positive punishment
B.
negative reinforcement; negative punishment
C.
negative punishment; negative reinforcement
D.
positive punishment; positive reinforcement
B. negative reinforcement; negative punishment

The rods in the retina are responsible for____ vision, and the cones are responsible for ____  vision.



A.
dim light; color
B.
color; bright light
C.
bright light; dim light
D.
color; dim light
A. dim light; color

A double blind study helps alleviate the problems caused by _. 



A.
Demand characteristics
B.
Falsifiability
C.
Independent variables
D.
Failure or replicate study
A. Demand characteristics

In classical conditioning, the diminishing of the CR following removal of the UCS is called ___.



A.
acquisition
B.
discrimination
C.
generalization
D.
extinction
D. extinction

Dian Fossey’s study of gorillas is an example of __. 



A.
a case study
B.
naturalistic observation that turned into participant observation
C.
participant observation
D.
naturalistic observation
D. naturalistic observation

Procedural memories are ______memories and thus are probably processed in the ________.



A.
explicit; hippocampus
B.
implicit; cerebellum
C.
explicit; cerebellum
D.
implicit; hippocampus
B. implicit; cerebellum

The stimulus in whose presence a response will be reinforced is called the _____ stimulus in operant conditioning.



A.
discriminative
B.
extinction
C.
acquisition
D.
generalization
A. discriminative

The stimulus in whose presence a response will be reinforced is called the _____ stimulus in operant conditioning.



A.
discriminative
B.
extinction
C.
acquisition
D.
generalization
A. discriminative

Even though the image of your dog on your retina changes as your dog runs to fetch a stick, you do not perceive your dog as getting smaller. Which process of perception explains this phenomenon?



A.
perceptual constancy
B.
perceptual set
C.
accommodation
D.
dark adaptation
A. perceptual constancy

Which of the following types of scatterplots depicts a weak, negative correlation?



A.
very little scatter with data points going from bottom left to top right
B.
very little scatter with data points going from top left to bottom right
C.
a lot of scatter with data points going from top left to bottom right
D.
a lot of scatter with data points going from bottom left to top right
C. a lot of scatter with data points going from top left to bottom right

The intelligence test scores for _ are most strongly correlated.



A.
unrelated people reared apart
B.
fraternal twins reared together
C.
siblings reared together
D.
identical twins reared apart
D. identical twins reared apart

Negative reinforcement occurs when an ____ stimulus is ___.



A.
appetitive; presented
B.
aversive; presented
C.
aversive; removed
D.
appetitive; removed
C. aversive; removed

A heritability estimate of 100% for intelligence in a given population means that the variation in intelligence for this population is determined ____ .






A. 
solely by genetics 
B. 
solely by environmental experiences 
C. 
50% by genetics and 50% by environmental experiences 
D. 
75% by genetics and 25% by environmental experiences
D. 75% by genetics and 25% by environmental experiences
